List of Funded AI Startups (2025)Chris WalkerJune 11, 2025  

AI startups are raising massive rounds and reshaping every industry.
From generative AI and machine learning infrastructure to vertical specific applications in healthcare, finance, and retail, newly funded AI startups are at the forefront of innovation. Backed by fresh capital, these companies are rapidly building teams, launching products, and looking for strategic partners to help them move faster.
At Fundraise Insider, we track every AI startup that secures funding and deliver direct contact details for the founders, CTOs, and heads of product, so you can engage early, when budgets are open and roadmaps are still being shaped.
👉 Get Funded Startup Leads like recently funded AI startups in Silicon Valley and across the country, every week. One Payment. Lifetime Leads. No subscription.

Here are the 19 US AI startups that have raised $100M or more in 2025 





Rebecca Szkutak





1:12 PM PDT · April 23, 2025 







 


 

 

 

 

 

 






Last year was monumental for the AI industry in the U.S. and beyond.
There were 49 startups that raised funding rounds worth $100 million or more in 2024, per our count at TechCrunch; three companies raised more than one “megaround,” and seven companies raised rounds $1 billion in size or larger.

Rebecca Szkutak





1:12 PM PDT · April 23, 2025 







 


 

 

 

 

 

 






Last year was monumental for the AI industry in the U.S. and beyond.
There were 49 startups that raised funding rounds worth $100 million or more in 2024, per our count at TechCrunch; three companies raised more than one “megaround,” and seven companies raised rounds $1 billion in size or larger.

StatesSunoMusic and sound generation$125 M2022CambridgeUnited StatesSynthesiaAI avatar and video generator$330 M2017LondonUnited KingdomThinking Machine LabsAI research company$0 M2024San FranciscoUnited StatesTogether AIAI cloud provider$534 M2022San FranciscoUnited StatesVannevar LabsDefense intelligence software$92 M2019Palo AltoUnited StatesVAST DataAI data infrastructure software$381 M2016New YorkUnited StatesWindsurfAI-powered coding tool$243 M2021Mountain ViewUnited StatesWorld LabsSpatial intelligence$292 M2024San FranciscoUnited StatesWriterEnterprise AI software$326 M2020San FranciscoUnited StatesXAIAI model developer$12.13 B2023Palo AltoUnited StatesInquire for List DataRequest license data and information spreadsheet from this Forbes Ranking for research, reference and other limited use options.Read MoreHow Forbes Compiled The 2025 AI 50 ListBy Elisabeth Brier  AI 50 2025: AI Agents Move Beyond ChatBy Konstantine Buhler  Why 300 Companies Use This Startup’s AI To Slash

How will 2025 compare? It’s still the first half of the year, but so far it looks like 2024’s momentum will continue this year. There have already been multiple billion-dollar rounds this year, and more AI megarounds closed in the U.S. in Q1 2025 compared to Q1 2024.
Here are all the U.S. AI companies that have raised $100 million this year:
April

SandboxAQ closed a $450 million Series E round on April 4 that valued the AI model company at $5.7 billion. The round included Nvidia, Google, and Bridgewater Associates founder Ray Dalio among other investors.
Runway, which creates AI models for media production, raised a $308 million Series D round that was announced on April 3, valuing the company at $3 billion. It was led by General Atlantic. SoftBank, Nvidia, and Fidelity also participated.

March

insights_extracted: In 2025, numerous AI startups secured significant funding, exceeding $100 million in some cases.
SandboxAQ raised $450 million in a Series E round in April 2025, valuing the company at $5.7 billion.
Runway, focusing on AI models for media production, raised $308 million in a Series D round in April 2025, reaching a $3 billion valuation.
Several other US AI companies raised over $100 million in funding during the first quarter of 2025.
The momentum of 2024's AI funding continued into 2025, with multiple billion-dollar rounds already completed by the first half of the year.
Many of these well-funded AI companies are developing generative AI, machine learning infrastructure, and vertical-specific applications across various industries (healthcare, finance, retail).

A Forbes list of AI companies includes entries like XAI (model developer, $12.13B funding in 2023), Together AI (cloud provider, $534M in 2022), and others.  Note that funding amounts listed are not necessarily from 2025.
TechCrunch reported on 19 US AI startups that raised $100M or more in 2025 (as of April 23rd, 2025).

AI (Artificial Intelligence) Startups funded by Y Combinator (YC) 2025 | Y Combinator

AI startup activity remains heavily concentrated in the US, with Silicon Valley (San Francisco, Palo Alto) alone accounting for over 25 % of all headquarters, an indicator of continued clustering around AI talent and capital. New York and Boston/Cambridge maintain strong ecosystems, particularly for healthcare and finance applications. Canada’s modest presence (Toronto, Montréal, Vancouver) points to emerging hubs, often linked to strong academic AI research centers, while isolated outliers (e.g., El Salvador) suggest nascent global diffusion.

Runway, an AI model company for media production, raised $308 million in Series D funding, reaching a $3 billion valuation in 2025.

AI startups are receiving significant funding across various sectors including healthcare, finance, and retail.
Many AI startups in 2025 are focused on generative AI and machine learning infrastructure.
Autonomous agents are a leading trend in seed funding for AI startups in 2025.

content_from_urls.content:
AI Dominance Continues
Artificial Intelligence remains the dominant investment theme, with 8 of the 15 largest funding rounds going to AI-focused startups. Companies like Together AI ($305M), Harvey ($300M), and Abridge ($250M) are pioneering applications across various industries:

Enterprise AI continues to attract major funding, with companies focusing on legal tech, healthcare, and IT infrastructure
Generative AI platforms are securing significant late-stage funding, indicating investor confidence in their long-term potential
Hardware infrastructure supporting AI development (like EnCharge AI’s $100M round) shows the comprehensive ecosystem being built around artificial intelligence

Deep Tech Gaining Traction
Beyond pure software plays, deep tech companies in quantum computing (QuEra Computing’s $230M), space technology (K2 Space’s $110M), and robotics (Apptronik’s $350M) secured significant funding.

Here are the 19 US AI startups that have raised $100M or more in 2025 





Rebecca Szkutak





1:12 PM PDT · April 23, 2025 







 


 

 

 

 

 

 






Last year was monumental for the AI industry in the U.S. and beyond.
There were 49 startups that raised funding rounds worth $100 million or more in 2024, per our count at TechCrunch; three companies raised more than one “megaround,” and seven companies raised rounds $1 billion in size or larger.

AI Investment Landscape in 2025: Opportunities in a Volatile Market









AI Investment Landscape in 2025: Opportunities in a Volatile Market
AI Investment Stays Strong in 2025, With a Shift Toward Practical Value and Financial Results






April 17, 2025
Downloads

Share

Footnotes: 
1: Nizar Tarhuni and Dylan Cox, “Q4 2024 PitchBook-NVCA Venture Monitor”, PitchBook (January 13, 2025).
2: Ibid 
3: Nizar Tarhuni and Dylan Cox, “Q4 2024 PitchBook-NVCA Venture Monitor”, PitchBook (January 13, 2025). Jacob Robbins, “AI startups grabbed a third of global VC dollars in 2024”, PitchBook (January 9, 2025). 
4: IDC, “Asia/Pacific* AI Investments to Reach $110 Billion by 2028, IDC Reports”, IDC (September 23, 2024), . 
5: Jacob Robbins, “Nearly 1 in 4 new startups is an AI company”, PitchBook (December 24, 2024), . 
6: Sumeet Gupta and Jiva J. Jagtap, “AI Takes Center-Stage for Value Creation in Private Equity Firms”, FTI Consulting Survey (2024). 
7: Sumeet Gupta, Samuel Aguirre, and Carl Jones, “Assisting a Leading Global Investment Fund With Strategic Review of an Advanced AI Platform-Based Company”, FTI Consulting (February 28, 2025).

AI Dominance Continues
Artificial Intelligence remains the dominant investment theme, with 8 of the 15 largest funding rounds going to AI-focused startups. Companies like Together AI ($305M), Harvey ($300M), and Abridge ($250M) are pioneering applications across various industries:

Enterprise AI continues to attract major funding, with companies focusing on legal tech, healthcare, and IT infrastructure
Generative AI platforms are securing significant late-stage funding, indicating investor confidence in their long-term potential
Hardware infrastructure supporting AI development (like EnCharge AI’s $100M round) shows the comprehensive ecosystem being built around artificial intelligence

Deep Tech Gaining Traction
Beyond pure software plays, deep tech companies in quantum computing (QuEra Computing’s $230M), space technology (K2 Space’s $110M), and robotics (Apptronik’s $350M) secured significant funding.

List of Funded AI Startups (2025)Chris WalkerJune 11, 2025  

AI startups are raising massive rounds and reshaping every industry.
From generative AI and machine learning infrastructure to vertical specific applications in healthcare, finance, and retail, newly funded AI startups are at the forefront of innovation. Backed by fresh capital, these companies are rapidly building teams, launching products, and looking for strategic partners to help them move faster.
At Fundraise Insider, we track every AI startup that secures funding and deliver direct contact details for the founders, CTOs, and heads of product, so you can engage early, when budgets are open and roadmaps are still being shaped.
👉 Get Funded Startup Leads like recently funded AI startups in Silicon Valley and across the country, every week. One Payment. Lifetime Leads. No subscription.

insights_extracted: In 2025, Together AI received $305M, Harvey received $300M, and Abridge received $250M in funding, pioneering AI applications across various industries.
At least 19 US AI startups raised $100M or more in 2025.
Enterprise AI, focusing on legal tech, healthcare, and IT infrastructure, attracted significant funding in 2025.
Generative AI platforms secured substantial late-stage funding in 2025.
AI hardware infrastructure companies, such as EnCharge AI ($100M), also received significant investment in 2025.
Other deep tech areas like quantum computing (QuEra Computing, $230M), space technology (K2 Space, $110M), and robotics (Apptronik, $350M) saw substantial funding in 2025.
AI investment remained strong in 2025, with a shift towards practical value and financial results.

insights_extracted: In 2025, AI startups are securing significant funding and are reshaping various industries.  These startups are focusing on generative AI, machine learning infrastructure, and vertical-specific applications in healthcare, finance, and retail.  AI startups received a significant portion of global VC dollars in 2024, and this trend appears to be continuing into 2025.  AI investment remains strong in 2025, with a shift towards practical value and financial results.  A substantial number of new startups in 2024 were AI companies.  Several sources list funded AI startups in 2025, but specific company names are not provided in the accessible text.

llm_interactions.response:
In 2025, numerous AI startups secured significant funding, exceeding $100 million in some cases.
SandboxAQ raised $450 million in a Series E round in April 2025, valuing the company at $5.7 billion.
Runway, focusing on AI models for media production, raised $308 million in a Series D round in April 2025, reaching a $3 billion valuation.
Several other US AI companies raised over $100 million in funding during the first quarter of 2025.
The momentum of 2024's AI funding continued into 2025, with multiple billion-dollar rounds already completed by the first half of the year.
Many of these well-funded AI companies are developing generative AI, machine learning infrastructure, and vertical-specific applications across various industries (healthcare, finance, retail).
A Forbes list of AI companies includes entries like XAI (model developer, $12.13B funding in 2023), Together AI (cloud provider, $534M in 2022), and others.  Note that funding amounts listed are not necessarily from 2025.
TechCrunch reported on 19 US AI startups that raised $100M or more in 2025 (as of April 23rd, 2025).
Several sources offer lists of funded AI startups in 2025, but full lists require payment or subscription access.
Y Combinator also funded AI startups in 2025, though a specific list and funding amounts are not readily available from the provided source.
